US Rental Investment Markets

Zillow data as of: February 28, 2026

Where should you buy rental property in the US? This page ranks 3803 cities by investment metrics derived from Zillow’s public home value and rent indices plus Census demand signals.

Jump to: Cash Flow Calculator · Leveraged Returns · Hidden Gems · Investor Rules · Composite Score · State Explorer · Price vs Rent Growth · City Lookup

The price-to-rent ratio is the standard cash-flow benchmark:

  • Strong Buy (P/R < 15): Cash flow positive from day one
  • Moderate (P/R 15–20): Reasonable returns, some appreciation upside
  • Lean Rent (P/R 20–25): Marginal; depends on appreciation
  • Rent Favored (P/R > 25): Buy only if you’re betting on price growth

Data Sources

All data is traceable and reproducible. Run Rscript scripts/us_rental_markets_data.R to refresh.

Data Sources
All public, no API key required
Source Description Updated
Zillow ZHVI Typical home values (city level, SFR+condo, 35th–65th percentile) 2026-02-28
Zillow ZORI Typical observed rents (city level, SFR+condo+MFR) 2026-02-28
Census Pop Estimates City + county population (2020–2024), net migration V2024
Census ACS 5-Year Vacancy rates, renter %, housing counts (county level) 2019–2023
Cap rate = gross yield × (1 − 35% expense ratio). Raw data: us_rental_markets.csv

Interactive Cash Flow Calculator

Plug in your financing assumptions and rerank every market by monthly cash flow, cash-on-cash return, and whether it actually clears the mortgage. Defaults are 25% down, 7.25% 30-yr fixed, 35% operating expenses, 3% closing costs.

# City State Price Rent/mo Mortgage Cash flow/mo CoC % Cap rate Pop Pop growth Vacancy Tier

Gross Yield vs Home Value

Leveraged Returns (at 25% Down, 7.25% 30yr)

Cap rate ignores financing. For the leveraged buyer the two numbers that matter are monthly cash flow (does the tenant cover the mortgage?) and cash-on-cash return (what does my out-of-pocket cash actually earn?). Both are computed at the default assumptions printed above – use the calculator for your own numbers.

Demand Health Check: Cap Rate vs Population Growth

High cap rate means nothing if the town is emptying out. This chart cross-references cap rate with population growth – the sweet spot is the upper-right quadrant: strong cash flow and growing population.

Hidden Gems: Small Towns with Big Cash Flow

A common investor strategy is to buy in smaller markets where price is low but rent holds up. The Hidden Gem flag filters for:

  • Population 5K–50K
  • Estimated cap rate ≥ 5%
  • Non-negative population growth
  • County vacancy rate ≤ 12%
## ** 91 cities** match the Hidden Gem criteria.
Show hidden gems

Investor Rules of Thumb

Two rent-to-price benchmarks investors use as a quick smell test:

  • 1% rule: Monthly rent ≥ 1% of purchase price. A pass usually means cash flow is plausible.
  • 2% rule: Monthly rent ≥ 2% of purchase price. Rare; typically only in distressed or low-priced markets.

Vacancy Rate vs Cap Rate

Markets with high vacancy rates are risky – your rental sits empty longer. Cities in the lower-right have strong cap rates and low vacancy.

Full US Market Explorer

Search, filter, and sort all 3803 cities.

Show full table

Composite Investment Score

A rank-based composite (0–100) that blends the signals investors actually care about: cap rate (35%), population growth (20%), net migration (15%), low vacancy (15%), and YoY price appreciation (15%). Each factor is converted to a percentile so outliers can’t dominate.

Top 50 by composite score

State Leaderboard

Best cash-flow city per state (highest monthly cash flow at default assumptions), plus state-level medians.

Yield Minus Mortgage Rate Spread

Gross rental yield minus the current mortgage rate (7.25%). Positive spread means the rent covers debt service with room to spare; negative means you’re subsidizing the mortgage and relying on appreciation.

Price vs Rent Growth (YoY)

Home value growth and rent growth tell different stories. When home prices outrun rents, cap rates compress (bad for investors); when rents outrun prices, cap rates expand.

Per-City Detail Lookup

Search for any city to see the full investor profile in one place.

Show full detail table

Tier Distribution

Metro-Level Comparison

Average metrics across cities within each metro area (metros with 5+ cities).

Show metro table

How to update this data: Run Rscript scripts/us_rental_markets_data.R to download Zillow ZHVI + ZORI. Then run Rscript scripts/us_rental_demand_data.R to merge Census population / vacancy / migration and compute leverage metrics (mortgage payment, cash flow, cash-on-cash, composite score). Then re-render this page.
